what: Fix LB correctness: stop caching output buffer, allocate fresh each call
why: LB recheck=True uses new random inputs each iteration. Cached output buffer may retain stale data from mla_decode_fwd internal state (get_meta_param LRU cache holds num_kv_splits_indptr tensors that may be modified in-place by stage1 ASM kernel, or output buffer not fully overwritten in edge cases). Only bs=256/kv=8192 fails because it uniquely uses num_kv_splits=1 with fp8 (stage1 writes directly to output, no stage2 reduce). Allocating output fresh eliminates any stale buffer interaction.
